purpose: Compact corpus-level boundary discipline for public-safe material, pointer-only references, excluded classes, and explicitly quarantined trees.
core_rule: Public corpus surfaces may describe, point to, classify, or route; they must not silently import unsafe runtime material into the public-safe layer.

boundary_classes.meaning: Public canonical artifacts and control surfaces intentionally published as corpus sources.
boundary_classes.allowed_public_treatment: Cite, classify, route, verify, and use as bounded public source.
boundary_classes.disallowed_treatment: Treat nearby private or runtime material as implicitly included with it.

boundary_classes.meaning: Public companion, narrative, or contextual material intentionally released as bounded companion.
boundary_classes.allowed_public_treatment: Cite as companion, route as companion, and describe its bounded role.
boundary_classes.disallowed_treatment: Read as automatic normative primary or witness proof.

boundary_classes.meaning: Public implementation-facing skeleton or closure material intentionally published with bounded scope.
boundary_classes.allowed_public_treatment: Cite as implementation proof or bounded runtime-facing context.
boundary_classes.disallowed_treatment: Treat as doctrinal owner or as permission to import adjacent private runtime trees.

boundary_classes.meaning: A domain that may be acknowledged as existing without being imported into corpus meaning.
boundary_classes.allowed_public_treatment: Mention owner, role, boundary fact, or pointer only.
boundary_classes.disallowed_treatment: Read, summarize, copy from, or use as canonical source.

boundary_classes.meaning: Private state, internal runtime traces, operator-sensitive configuration, or continuity-sensitive operational data.
boundary_classes.allowed_public_treatment: At most acknowledge abstract class or boundary fact.
boundary_classes.disallowed_treatment: Ingest, quote, classify as public source, or promote into public verification surfaces.

boundary_classes.boundary_class: dangerous / non-public-safe code or operational content
boundary_classes.meaning: Code or operational material whose public corpus inclusion would be unsafe or irresponsible.
boundary_classes.allowed_public_treatment: Mention only at high level if boundary explanation requires it.
boundary_classes.disallowed_treatment: Publish operational detail, executable guidance, or code as public corpus content.

boundary_classes.boundary_class: excluded mixed environment / live entity domain
boundary_classes.meaning: A mixed domain where public-safe and live/private material coexist without reliable separation.
boundary_classes.allowed_public_treatment: Keep excluded or pointer-only until explicitly separated and classified.
boundary_classes.disallowed_treatment: Cherry-pick from the mixed domain as if it were clean public-safe corpus.

boundary_classes.meaning: A tree explicitly designated off-limits by owner or operator.
boundary_classes.allowed_public_treatment: Acknowledge only as a quarantine fact if already known from safe surfaces.
boundary_classes.disallowed_treatment: Read, diff, baseline, validate paths against, copy from, or derive corpus layers from it.

excluded_material_classes.public_safe_treatment_rule: Exclude from corpus sourcing; at most acknowledge as pointer-only boundary fact.

excluded_material_classes.material_class: deployment-sensitive code not intended for public corpus
excluded_material_classes.public_safe_treatment_rule: Do not import and do not restate as public implementation canon.

excluded_material_classes.material_class: private keys / secrets / credentials / access tokens
excluded_material_classes.public_safe_treatment_rule: Exclude absolutely; never copy, quote, hash, or summarize.
Exclude; only abstract class mention is allowed.

excluded_material_classes.material_class: unsafe automation or public-dangerous code surfaces
excluded_material_classes.public_safe_treatment_rule: Exclude from public corpus; only bounded high-level classification is allowed.

excluded_material_classes.material_class: mixed public/private mirrors without explicit classification
excluded_material_classes.public_safe_treatment_rule: Treat as excluded until explicit classification separates safe from unsafe.
Keep excluded or pointer-only until explicit public-safe release exists.

excluded_material_classes.material_class: any source tree explicitly marked off-limits by owner/operator
excluded_material_classes.public_safe_treatment_rule: Quarantine completely; do not use for baseline, diff, reference, or derivation.

pointer_only_rule: Some non-public-safe domains may be acknowledged only as boundary facts, without reading from them, copying from them, or using them as canonical source.
quarantine_rule: If a source tree is designated off-limits, it is not to be used for baseline, diff, reference, path validation, or corpus-layer derivation.
intake_interaction: New package intake must first determine whether candidate material is public-safe, pointer-only, or excluded before any corpus integration decision proceeds.
fail_closed_rule: If maintainers cannot establish that a source or artifact is public-safe for corpus inclusion, treat it as excluded or pointer-only until clarified.
